messages.text: Yesterday: PROJ-118 in review. Today: starting INFRA-56 rate limiter work, continuing SSO. No blockers.
Yesterday: usage dashboard layout scaffolded. Today: wiring up the charts, then picking up PROJ-130 dark mode.
Yesterday: nightly backups merged. Today: chasing down the flaky integration test suite (INFRA-60), it's blocking PR merges.
No blockers, reviewing PRs. Heads up sprint 15 planning is next Wednesday 2 PM.
e2e login tests PR is up (#140). Also found a rendering bug on Safari, filing BUG-207 now.
Yesterday: PROJ-118 merged, started INFRA-56. Today: fixing BUG-207 Safari rendering bug, then back to SSO review comments. No blockers.
Yesterday: usage dashboard charts wired up. Today: PROJ-130 dark mode PR, and looking at the CI snapshot failures on #147.
Root-caused the flaky CI issue, fix incoming today. Also starting on connection pooling (INFRA-63) — DB latency has been spiking.
Watching the DB latency thing closely, will escalate if it doesn't stabilize by this afternoon.
Filed BUG-211 (timezone offset bug in calendar widget) and BUG-208 (mobile export button). Otherwise continuing test coverage on dark mode.
Reminder: standup is 10 AM sharp today, sprint planning right after lunch at 2 PM. Please skim PROJ-140/PROJ-141 before then.
Heads up — no standup this Friday, I'll be at the offsite. Please post async updates in this channel instead.
Added a quick Loom walkthrough of the dark mode toggle for anyone curious: link in #engineering.
Nice, will check it out before I finish the test PR.
@aisha.khan left some comments on #136 (SSO login) — mainly the refresh token isn't invalidating the old one on rotation. Also `test_token_refresh_rotation` looks flaky in CI, can you take a look?
Yep, on it — will push a fix for the rotation logic and dig into the flaky test this morning.
Reminder this is needed for the Vector Analytics demo Friday morning — thank you both for the quick turnaround!
INFRA-56 (rate limiter) is blocking my read-replica rollout and Rahul's dashboard load testing. @marcus.webb can we get infra change approval today?
Approving now, go ahead and merge once CI is green. @aisha.khan the flaky test on #64 is the same root cause devon just fixed in INFRA-60, might just need a rebase.
Found the CI flakiness cause — test DB container wasn't fully ready before the suite started. Adding a proper healthcheck wait, PR incoming.
That explains the intermittent failures on my rate limiter PR too, thank you!
PROJ-118 notification refactor is merged. Nice cleanup @aisha.khan, this should also kill the duplicate-notification bug (BUG-209).
Great timing, that duplicate notification bug has been reported twice by support this month.
PROJ-130 dark mode PR (#144) is up, tagged @aisha.khan for a look at the preference-storage endpoint.
Will review after I finish the Safari fix today.
CI is failing on #147 (usage dashboard) — snapshot tests. Not sure what changed.
Looks like the chart legend markup changed in a recent charting-lib bump — snapshots are stale. Left a comment on the PR, should just need fixture updates.
Ah that makes sense, updating fixtures now. Ticket's due tomorrow so hoping to land it today.
Opened an experimental PR for server-side rendering on the dashboard (#141) — curious if it's worth the added complexity.
Ran the numbers — hydration cost outweighs the TTFB win for our typical data sizes. I'd shelve this for now.
Agreed, closing it. Good experiment to have run though.
Bumping react to 18.3 (#142), no breaking API changes for us, just picks up some concurrent-rendering fixes.
LGTM, approved.
Opened #137 adding HMAC signature verification for inbound webhooks — currently anyone can hit that endpoint unauthenticated. @devon.brooks mind reviewing given the infra/security overlap?
On my list, will get to it today or tomorrow.

Sprint 15 planning is Wed 2 PM. Agenda: review velocity from Sprint 14, scope PROJ-140 (workspace switcher) and PROJ-141 (SAML support), and talk through the INFRA-56/BUG-199 carryover. Please skim the tickets beforehand.
I'll bring client priorities for next quarter too, mainly around enterprise SSO/SAML demand.
BUG-207: dashboard charts render completely blank on Safari 17.4/17.5, macOS Sonoma. Chrome and Firefox are fine. Screenshots attached in the ticket.
Confirmed repro. Looks like canvas width is 0 on first paint under Safari's layout timing — investigating today.
Fix is up in #143 with a regression test. @priya.nair can you re-verify on your Safari setup once it's merged?

@here Prod dashboard query latency is spiking to 8-12s during the 10-11 AM PT peak window. Multiple customers affected, including Vector Analytics. Filed as BUG-199 (critical), @devon.brooks investigating.
Confirmed it's connection exhaustion on the primary DB, not a query plan regression. Working on adding pgbouncer connection pooling now (INFRA-63).
@aisha.khan can you help review #63 (connection pooling) today? Vector Analytics has emailed twice about this, want to get it out fast.
Will review right after standup.
Support flagged a flash-of-unstyled-content issue on the login page from a customer on a slow connection. Filed BUG-210.
Reproduced it, it's a CSS loading-order issue. Fix in progress, PR up as #146.
BUG-211: calendar widget meeting times are off by an hour for CET users during DST. Looks like a stale UTC offset table. Assigning to @aisha.khan since it's the shared timezone-resolution module.
Thanks for the clear repro steps, queued for this sprint.
BUG-212: the legacy ad-hoc CSV export silently truncates at 10k rows with no warning. Found while testing a large workspace.
This'll be superseded by the new streaming export in PROJ-125 — I'll close it once that ships instead of patching the legacy path.
BUG-213 (websocket memory leak) is resolved and merged. Root cause was leaked listener references on long-lived connections — added a cleanup on disconnect plus a load test to catch regressions.
Great find, that one had been nagging us for weeks.
BUG-208: report export button looks enabled but is unclickable below 480px width. Assigning to @rahul.verma.
Got it, will pick this up after the usage dashboard PR.
Weekly bug triage: BUG-199 (critical, in progress) and BUG-207 (high, in progress) are top priority. BUG-211 and BUG-208 are this-sprint. BUG-212 waits on PROJ-125.
The office coffee machine is making concerning noises again
third time this month lol
Welcome to Kavya, joining the support team today! Say hi when you see her around.
anyone else watching the match tonight?
yep, should be a good one
found this and thought of our connection pool incident: 'it's not a bug, it's a limited resource' 😂
too real
Hey — can you confirm SSO login will be demo-ready for the Vector Analytics call Friday morning? Want to loop in Tom Reyes if so.
Should be — just addressing the last review comments from Marcus, aiming to merge by Thursday EOD.
Given BUG-199 is critical, feel free to pause SSO work for a bit if the pooling PR (#63) needs backend eyes today.
Got it, will review #63 right after standup and prioritize accordingly.
Heads up — Postgres upgrade (INFRA-62) needs a short maintenance window this weekend, likely Saturday 2-3 AM IST. Will confirm exact timing once BUG-199 is fully resolved.
testing golden task suite
golden task regression check

**Yesterday**
- Merged PROJ-118 (notification refactor) and kicked off INFRA-56 (API gateway rate limiting).
- Repro'd and started investigating BUG-207 (Safari chart rendering bug — canvas width 0 on first paint).
- Got review feedback from Marcus on PROJ-101 (SSO login) around token refresh handling.

**Today**
- Shipped a fix for BUG-207 — PR #143 (pulse-web) is up with a regression test, approved by Rahul, CI passing.
- Addressing PROJ-101 review comments: fixing refresh-token invalidation and looking into the flaky `test_token_refresh_rotation` CI test (PR #136, changes requested).
- Kicking off PROJ-125 (CSV export) — streaming export endpoint work starts in today's focus block.
- Reviewing devon.brooks' pulse-infra#63 (connection pooling, BUG-199 incident fix) after standup, plus attending the BUG-199 status check and INFRA-63 code review session.
- Sprint 15 planning this afternoon (scoping PROJ-140/141, BUG-199/INFRA-56 carryover).

**Blockers**
- INFRA-56: rate limiter PR #64 has failing CI and is awaiting review; per Devon's comment it's blocking his INFRA-64 read-replica rollout and Rahul's load testing, pending infra change approval.
- PROJ-101: PR #136 blocked on changes requested (token refresh fix + flaky test), with Sarah pushing for merge by Thursday EOD ahead of Friday's client demo.
- No other blockers reported.

**Standup — Aisha Khan (Backend, Platform)**

**Yesterday**
- Merged PROJ-118 (notification refactor, PR #134) and kicked off INFRA-56 (API gateway rate limiting).
- Reproduced BUG-207 (Safari chart rendering — canvas width 0 on first paint).
- Got review feedback from Marcus on PROJ-101 (SSO login, PR #136) around token refresh handling.

**Today**
- Shipped the BUG-207 fix — PR #143 (pulse-web) has a regression test, approved by Rahul, CI passing.
- Working through PROJ-101 review comments: fixing refresh-token invalidation and the flaky `test_token_refresh_rotation` test (PR #136).
- Kicked off PROJ-125 (CSV export) — starting the streaming export endpoint in today's focus block.
- Reviewing Devon's connection-pooling PR (#63, INFRA-63) for the BUG-199 incident, plus attending the BUG-199 status check and INFRA-63 code review session.
- Sprint 15 planning this afternoon — scoping PROJ-140/141 and carryover from BUG-199/INFRA-56.

**Blockers**
- INFRA-56: rate limiter PR #64 has failing CI and is awaiting review; per Devon, this is blocking his INFRA-64 read-replica rollout and Rahul's load testing.
- PROJ-101: PR #136 is blocked on changes requested (token refresh fix + flaky test); Sarah is pushing for a merge by Thursday EOD ahead of Friday's client demo.
